blocks.text: was already watching the house next door when she reached for her bag. She didn't say anything about it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Okay. The view from the back is actually incredible. Come look.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I'll be there in a minute.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You said that and then you stood in the doorway for five minutes.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I was looking at the layout.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Do you want to walk the beach before it gets dark? We have time.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It was a yes or no question.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You usually find a third option.

blocks.prompt: He said yes immediately. Does he know why?

options.label: Yes. It wasn't about the beach.
The beach. He genuinely wanted to see it.
He doesn't examine it. Some things are better left alone.
